data.title: Erie Insurance CEO Tim NeCastro to retire at end of 2026 after a 30-year career. Search for new CEO begins.
data.description: Erie Insurance (Nasdaq: ERIE) disclosed today that Tim NeCastro will step down as president and CEO at the end of 2026 after 30 years with the company. The CEO search will commence promptly to fill the impending vacancy.

data.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump's tariffs, causing 10 stocks to soar. Court rules 6-3 against tariffs. Congress has power to impose duties and taxes.
data.description: In a historic decision, the Supreme Court invalidated President Donald Trump's extensive global tariffs, leading to a significant surge in U.S. equities on Friday. Investors welcomed the elimination of duties that had significantly impacted trade patterns and markets for almost a year. The ruling, announced on Friday, stated that the International Emergency Economic Powers Act does not give the president the authority to impose tariffs. Chief Justice John Roberts wrote that only Congress, as outlined in Article I of the Constitution, has the power to implement duties and taxes. In a combined 6-3 decision, the Court clarified that the authority granted by the IEEPA to "regulate... importation" does not extend to imposing tariffs. The majority opinion emphasized that the president must have "clear congressional authorization" to justify such broad economic measures. President Trump had invoked national emergencies related to drug trafficking and significant trade imbalances to justify imposing a 25% duty.

data.title: BioNTech files lawsuit against Moderna over next-gen COVID-19 shot, claiming patent infringement on mNEXSPIKE vaccine.
data.description: BioNTech SE has filed a patent infringement lawsuit against Moderna Inc., alleging that Moderna's mNEXSPIKE COVID-19 vaccine utilizes proprietary technology developed by BioNTech. BioNTech, known for its partnership with Pfizer in creating the Comirnaty vaccine, claims that Moderna is using its patented streamlined vaccine design without authorization. The lawsuit states that BioNTech's technology focuses on specific domains of the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein, resulting in a strong immune response at lower doses and improved storage stability. BioNTech secured intellectual property protection through U.S. Patent No. 12,133,899 issued in November 2024. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.
